title: MailerLite vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Omnisend. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool fits your business best.
intro: MailerLite and Omnisend are both popular freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different needs. MailerLite focuses on simplicity and affordability for small businesses, while Omnisend specializes in omnichannel marketing for e-commerce brands. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing strategy.

comparison_table.tool_a: Drag-drop editor, landing pages, surveys, automation
comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, push notifications, product picker, advanced segmentation

choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer on a tight budget with under 1,000 subscribers
You want a user-friendly, drag-and-drop editor that's easy to learn quickly
You need landing pages and surveys in addition to email marketing
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and want to combine email, SMS, and push notifications
You need advanced segmentation and pre-built automation workflows for product recommendations
You're willing to pay slightly more for a specialized omnichannel platform

faq.a: It depends on your needs. MailerLite is better for general email marketing simplicity and affordability, while Omnisend excels for e-commerce brands needing multi-channel capabilities. MailerLite has a higher G2 rating (4.6 vs 4.5), but Omnisend scores slightly higher on Trustpilot (4.6 vs 4.5).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Omnisend?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper at $9/month compared to Omnisend's $16/month for paid plans. Additionally, MailerLite's free tier supports 1,000 subscribers versus Omnisend's 250, making it the more budget-friendly option overall.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list exports and imports. Most of your subscriber data can be transferred between them, though you may need to rebuild automation workflows and design templates. Always back up your data before migrating.
